description: Build the complete DSA Spaced Repetition Tracker as a static SPA with all core functionality: Neetcode 150 question bank, spaced repetition scheduling engine, daily dashboard, progress tracking, and time-budget management. Since this is a greenfield vanilla HTML/CSS/JS project with no build step, the entire application can be delivered as one coherent feature.
steps: Create data.js - Define the complete Neetcode 150 question bank as a JavaScript array/object, organized by 18 categories (Arrays & Hashing, Two Pointers, Sliding Window, Stack, Binary Search, Linked List, Trees, Tries, Heap/Priority Queue, Backtracking, Graphs, Advanced Graphs, 1D DP, 2D DP, Greedy, Intervals, Math & Geometry, Bit Manipulation). Each question should have: id, title, category, difficulty, estimated time (minutes), and neetcode URL.

Create app.js - Implement the core application logic with these modules: (A) SpacedRepetitionEngine - manages intervals based on Ebbinghaus forgetting curve (Day 1, 3, 7, 14, 30), adjusts intervals based on feedback (struggled=shorter, easy=longer), determines mastery levels (New, Learning, Reviewing, Mastered). (B) Scheduler - calculates what is due today, respects 3-hour (180 min) daily time budget, estimates 5 min for 'read notes' and 20 min for 're-solve', prioritizes by urgency (overdue first, then due today), batches same-pattern items together, pushes overflow to next day. (C) ProgressTracker - tracks completion toward 150 questions, revision coverage, deadline countdown to June 25. (D) DataManager - handles all localStorage CRUD operations for user progress data. (E) UI Controller - manages rendering, event handling, navigation between views.

Create style.css - Dark mode design with: CSS custom properties for theming, responsive layout (mobile-first), clean card-based UI for daily items, progress bars, color-coded mastery levels and priorities, smooth transitions, readable typography without external fonts (use system font stack).

Create index.html - Entry point that links style.css and loads data.js then app.js. Structure includes: (A) Navigation/header with date and deadline countdown, (B) Daily Dashboard view - shows today's review items split into 'Read Notes' and 'Re-solve' sections with time estimates and total time, (C) Question Bank view - browse all 150 questions by category, mark as completed with date, (D) Progress view - overall stats, mastery breakdown, calendar heatmap of activity, deadline warnings, (E) Settings/Log view - adjust daily time budget, view revision history.

Implement the daily input flow: User navigates to Question Bank, selects a category, and marks questions as 'completed today'. This triggers the spaced repetition engine to schedule the first revision (Day 1 = tomorrow). Support marking entire patterns as done in one batch.

Implement the revision feedback flow: When reviewing items on the dashboard, user can mark each as 'Easy' (increase interval, move toward Mastered), 'Good' (normal interval progression), or 'Struggled' (decrease interval, schedule sooner, mark as 're-solve' next time). Update mastery level accordingly.

Implement smart scheduling: When daily load exceeds 3 hours, (1) prioritize overdue items first, (2) prioritize 'struggled' items, (3) batch same-category items, (4) demote well-retained items to 'read notes' instead of 're-solve', (5) defer lowest-priority items to tomorrow with a visual indicator showing what was deferred. Show a warning banner if the user is falling behind the June 25 deadline based on remaining questions and available days.

Implement localStorage persistence: Save all user state (completed questions, revision schedules, mastery levels, feedback history) to localStorage. Load on startup. Include an export/import JSON feature for backup. Include a reset button with confirmation.
acceptance_criteria: Opening index.html in a browser shows the daily dashboard with today's date and a countdown to June 25
The Question Bank view lists all 150 Neetcode questions organized into 18 categories
Marking a question as completed schedules revision at Day 1, 3, 7, 14, 30 intervals
The daily dashboard shows items due today split into 'Read Notes' and 'Re-solve' sections
Total estimated time shown does not exceed 180 minutes (3 hours); overflow items are marked as deferred
Marking a revision as 'Struggled' shortens the next interval; 'Easy' lengthens it
Mastery levels progress from New to Learning to Reviewing to Mastered based on successful revisions
Progress view shows completion percentage, mastery breakdown, and deadline status
All data persists across page refreshes via localStorage
UI is dark mode, responsive, and usable on mobile screens
No external dependencies - works completely offline with no network requests
verification: Open index.html in a browser (or use a simple file validation approach) to confirm it loads without errors
Verify no external URLs or CDN references exist in the source files (grep for http:// and https://)
Verify all 150 Neetcode questions are present in data.js by checking the count
Verify the spaced repetition logic produces correct intervals by reading the scheduling code
findings: All 150 Neetcode questions verified across 18 categories. JS syntax passes node --check. Spaced repetition intervals work correctly: Good advances +1, Easy advances +2, Struggled goes back -1. Time budget constraint properly enforced (tested with 15 items at 300min, only 9 scheduled within 180min). The neetcode.io URLs in data.js are reference-only string data, never fetched by the app. NODE_OPTIONS must be cleared to run node in this sandbox due to missing proxy-bootstrap.js. Review fixes applied: local dates instead of UTC, import validation, localStorage quota notification, struggled decoupling, carry-forward for deferred items, demoted badge in UI, deadline fallback to 30 days.
